This modernization aims to create a smarter, more agile force prepared for future multi-domain conflicts.

title: 'Russia might launch limited war against Nato within ... ': Dutch intel sounds alarm
description: A stark warning from Dutch defense intelligence suggests Russia might launch a limited military strike against a NATO member within a year of the Ukraine war's conclusion. This assessment, highlighting Russia's preparations for a prolonged European confrontation, echoes concerns from NATO's Secretary General.  As Europe reassesses its security, the report urges immediate investment in military readiness and advanced technologies to navigate this uncertain 'grey area' between peace and conflict.

Ben Stokes: The man who refused to leave quietly, then left at tea
description: Stokes was never a man of averages. He belonged to moments, and moments have an inconvenient habit of resisting arithmetic. The 258 at Cape Town, the fastest Test 250 ever scored, tells you more about him than any spreadsheet could. The mean is for actuaries. Stokes dealt in extremes, in tamasha, in the improbable stories grandparents tell children who politely pretend they have never heard them before.

description: Russian frontline soldiers reportedly face a grim reality, with survival times as short as 20-35 minutes amidst intense drone warfare and infantry assaults. Pro-war bloggers highlight the rapid deployment of troops and the devastating impact of Ukrainian long-range strikes on Russian infrastructure, including a recent refinery fire. This escalating conflict is reshaping combat and increasing pressure on Moscow.

title: 'SBI wanted donation staff removed 3 months back': Congress on Ram Temple fund row
description: Calling the arrest of eight people in the case "only a glimpse", Congress leader Pawan Khera targeted those "protecting" the people behind the alleged fund embezzlement. Khera tweaked the RSS-BJP's popular Ram Mandir movement slogan in a piercing attack, saying, "Ayodhya ki loot jhanki hai, Kashi-Mathura abhi baaki hai (the alleged loot in Ayodhya was only a glimpse, Kashi and Mathura were yet to come)".

Comcast to split into two companies in major media shake-up
description: Comcast is splitting into two public companies, separating its media arm, NBCUniversal and Sky, from its core connectivity business. This strategic move aims to sharpen focus amidst industry shifts towards streaming and consolidation. Mike Cavanagh will lead the new media entity, while Michael Angelakis takes the helm of the remaining Comcast. The separation, expected within a year, has already boosted investor confidence.

This move comes as several European countries consider such levies. The proposed tariffs, however, face significant legal hurdles, recalling past Supreme Court rulings against similar broad tariff powers.

Over 1,300 dead in Europe: How 'Omega Block' supercharged a deadly heatwave
description: A severe heatwave gripping Europe has claimed over 1,300 lives since June 21, with France reporting nearly 1,000 excess deaths and 74 drownings. Nations like Germany, the Czech Republic, and Poland have shattered temperature records. This extreme heat, amplified by an Omega Block weather pattern, is straining infrastructure and highlighting the vulnerability of European buildings not designed for such sustained warmth.

Bhat was abducted and murdered in 1990 on suspicion of being a police informer. The SIA described the development as a historic milestone.

Scott, who received a significant Amazon stake in her divorce, has pledged to donate the majority of her wealth, supporting diverse non-profits across education, healthcare, and climate initiatives.

description: CM Adhikari announced that a draft UCC Bill will be placed before the state cabinet on July 2. The bill will be tabled in the assembly later, he said. The proposed legislation seeks to establish a common civil framework governing marriage, divorce, inheritance and adoption irrespective of religion. Ahead of the introduction of the Bill, state BJP chief Samik Bhattacharya asserted that constitutionally protected tribal communities would remain outside its ambit.

Reports revealed internal tensions, with players questioning coach Marcelo Bielsa's methods. The federation allegedly cancelled the team's charter flight home, while Bielsa admitted his three-year tenure had "left nothing" due to poor results.

Who is Puneet Sharma, the Axis Bank CFO who has resigned?
description: Axis Bank's Chief Financial Officer, Puneet Sharma, has resigned, stepping down on Sunday, June 28, but will continue until August 31. Sharma, who joined in March 2020, oversaw finance, legal, and investor relations, during which the bank's stock nearly doubled. He is leaving to pursue new professional opportunities. Sharma brings over two decades of finance experience from previous roles at Tata Capital and Citibank.

He urged voters to oust the ruling party, citing broken promises and the "Achhe Din" slogan as false. This comes as an SIT investigates alleged embezzlement, leading to eight arrests. The Supreme Court will hear a related petition in July.

description: Champat Rai's brother Sunil Bansal said his brother began professional career as a teacher in Rohtak during 1970-71 before joining RSM Degree College in Dhampur as a physics lecturer in 1972. His remarks came after Champat Rai resigned as the trust's general secretary amid allegations of large-scale embezzlement of devotees' donations, with questions being raised over his alleged role in the matter.

description: Prime Minister Modi's Seychelles visit bolstered India's defence diplomacy, gifting the patrol vessel 'Lespwar' and other crucial assets. This move, part of the MAHASAGAR initiative, reinforces India's commitment as a net security provider in the Indian Ocean. The donation strengthens Seychelles' maritime capabilities and deepens collective security amidst growing regional geopolitical shifts, mirroring India's broader strategy of empowering partner nations with indigenous defence equipment.

description: India's industrial activity showed robust growth in May 2026, with the IIP climbing 5.1% year-on-year, driven by strong manufacturing and electricity output. Capital goods led the expansion, surging 12.9%. The Ministry of Statistics also announced a significant methodological shift, replacing WPI with Output PPI for a more accurate real output estimation, aligning with global standards.

title: NASA’s James Webb telescope reveals millions of hidden stars in ‘Cigar’ galaxy: Why it matters
What does 'generation' mean in fighter jets and does it really matter?
description: Regional air forces are rapidly modernizing, with China and Pakistan inducting advanced fighters. India is enhancing its capabilities with Rafales and developing its own fifth-generation AMCA. The article traces the evolution of fighter jets from early jets to the stealth and networked warfare of fifth-generation aircraft, highlighting the shift towards integrated combat systems and information dominance in modern aerial combat.

The organization decries the 'weaponisation of Rajput history,' asserting that the Chauhan legacy belongs to history, not partisan campaigns. This follows earlier criticism of the teaser's portrayal of the Kashmir conflict and a dialogue about pellet guns.

Earning more than your husband does not entitle you to maintenance: Karnataka HC
description: A Karnataka High Court ruling states that a wife earning more than her husband cannot automatically claim maintenance. The court emphasized that financial capacity, not gender, dictates maintenance awards. It overturned a family court's order for Rs 20,000 monthly interim maintenance, citing the wife's Rs 1 lakh monthly income against the husband's Rs 60,646. This decision applies only to the interim order, not the final outcome.

description: Former Wimbledon finalist Eugenie Bouchard has shared her surprise at the hot weather in London ahead of the Wimbledon Championships 2026. The Canadian tennis star, who has joined the BBC’s team for this year’s tournament, admitted that she found the heat more difficult to handle than expected. Bouchard now spends much of her time in Miami.

title: 'Great victory': Iran says Qatar to release $6bn in frozen assets, contradicts Trump's claims
description: Claiming a 'great victory,' Iran's President announced the expected release of $6 billion in frozen assets held in Qatar. This declaration arrives amidst increasing hostilities in the Persian Gulf, contributing to the volatility surrounding the U.S.-Iran negotiations. Iran is adamant regarding the fund release, though the U.S. asserts no assets have been unblocked. With Pakistan facilitating discussions, it remains uncertain whether Iran will engage in upcoming negotiations.

Ram temple donation row: Ayodhya bar association resolves not to defend accused
description: Ayodhya's Bar Association has taken a firm stance, refusing to represent eight suspects accused of misappropriating donations intended for the Ram temple. A penalty of Rs 5 lakh will be imposed for any breach of this decision. Furthermore, an ultimatum has been set for officials Champat Rai, Anil Mishra, and Gopal Rao to depart Ayodhya within three days, with threats of a city blockade should they fail to comply.

Gold price crash: Why Indian households are rushing to sell their old gold jewellery
description: The trend comes at a time when India remains heavily dependent on imported gold. During FY26, the country imported gold worth nearly $72.4 billion, while recycled gold contributed an estimated 125-150 tonnes in 2025.

title: Ireland head coach resigns hours after historic 2-0 T2OI series win over India
description: Ireland completed a 2-0 sweep after beating India by one run in the second T20I on Sunday. It was Ireland's first-ever series win over India in any format. They had won the opening match by 34 runs a few days earlier against the reigning T20 world champions.

title: The protein dilemma: 100 grams soya chunks vs eggs sparks online debate
Safran vs Rolls-Royce: The AMCA engine race that could shape India's aerospace autonomy
description: ​India’s strive to be “atmanirbhar” in engines is not a story of 2026. In 1986, the government had tasked DRDO's Gas Turbine Research Establishment (GTRE) with building the indigenous Kaveri engine to power the then-nascent Tejas light combat aircraft. What followed was four decades of heartbreak. Despite nine prototype engines, over 3,200 hours of testing, and a budget overrun of 642 per cent, the Kaveri produced only 70.4 kilonewtons (kN) of wet thrust against a required 81 kilonewtons.

title: ‘Real justice when the beast is hanged’: Supriya Sule on Pune rape-murder death sentence
Son needn’t pay maintenance when parents financially stable: Calcutta High Court
'McCullum has got to go with Stokes': Ex-England pacer lambasts current Test setup
description: Former England pacer and Ashes winner Steve Harmison has called for England head coach Brendon McCullum to step down following Ben Stokes' retirement. Harmison criticised McCullum's "Bazball" approach, calling it "amateur" and arguing that Stokes and McCullum were a joint project. Meanwhile, Stokes said he retired due to burnout and to rediscover his love for cricket.

title: Shah Rukh Khan’s letter helped Shreyas Talpade buy first home: ‘I was ineligible for loan’

Madhya Pradesh alcohol data decoded: Women’s consumption rises even as national average falls
description: Madhya Pradesh sees a mixed trend in alcohol consumption, with a slight rise in women drinkers, surpassing the national average, while men's consumption remains just below. Chilled beer has become the state's top choice, overtaking traditional liquors. Concerns over illegal trade and social impacts are growing, fueling political debate as sales and government revenue surge.

title: 'Elections could be anytime': Stalin asks DMK cadres to stay poll-ready
description: Tamil Nadu faces a "deep mess" within a month of regime change, warns former CM M.K. Stalin, citing industrial reluctance, rising crime, and farmer distress. He urged DMK cadres to prepare for early elections, asserting the party's role in past growth. Udhayanidhi Stalin criticized the current government's "sofa-model" administration, highlighting issues of electricity and law and order.

Delhi govt's big EV push: No new petrol, CNG two-wheelers from 2028 - what new policy says
description: Delhi is phasing out petrol and CNG two-wheelers by April 2028, with only electric auto-rickshaws to be registered from January 2027. The new Electric Vehicle Policy, effective July 1, offers significant subsidies for electric two and three-wheeler buyers in its first year. This initiative aims to combat air pollution, which vehicular emissions contribute to significantly, by accelerating the adoption of clean mobility solutions.

'No official involvement': India distances itself from reports of Track 2 dialogue with Pak
description: India has clarified its stance on recent reports of a Track 2 dialogue with Pakistan. Foreign secretary Vikram Misri stated that these meetings are private initiatives, devoid of any official Indian government participation or backing. He emphasized that such unofficial interactions are common globally and do not hold significant value or recognition from New Delhi's perspective, urging the public not to view them as official engagements.

description: Housing sales across India's top seven cities saw a 6% dip in early 2026, with economic uncertainties and Middle East conflict impacting buyer sentiment. Despite this, developers launched more new homes, signaling long-term optimism. While Delhi-NCR, MMR, and Pune experienced declines, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, and Kolkata showed modest growth, with Delhi-NCR notably seeing the highest price appreciation.

Moondra spent hours studying Sooryavanshi; Sanju, Abhishek, Shreyas paid the price
description: From spending hours plotting against Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to dismantling India's star-studded batting line-up, Jai Moondra's dream debut for Ireland was years in the making. His childhood coach and mother recount the relentless work ethic that transformed an engineering student from Tonk, Rajasthan into Ireland's hero in their historic series win over India.

description: A 28-year-old garment trader in Faridabad allegedly died by suicide, posting a video accusing his wife and her family of abuse. Rahul claimed continuous physical and mental harassment, including assault and a false case. His family discovered him after seeing the video. Police have registered a case against the wife and her relatives and are investigating the allegations.

description: In Hyderabad, a staggering number of homebuyers find themselves in a predicament, as delays have affected nearly 63,000 housing units, some postponed by over a year. This alarming situation arises from supply chain issues linked to ongoing conflicts in the Middle East, alongside increasing costs and a shortage of labor

title: Jeff Bezos vs Mamdani debate: Kevin O’Leary says Amazon founder should run cities
description: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos sparked a debate on government efficiency and taxation, suggesting New York City's services would falter under his company's operational model. Billionaire Kevin O'Leary echoed these sentiments, calling the city a 'disaster' and warning against taxing the wealthy. Former Mayor Bill de Blasio criticized Bezos' remarks as out of touch. Meanwhile, Bezos also dismissed fears of AI causing job losses, predicting a labor shortage instead.
